fields.description:
## Why
dso's deepest lesson is the LOOP: overturned findings become named guards and eval cases. Plus two small
reliability wins: visibility of review effort/health, and a whole-diff signal for agent consumers.

## What
- FP-ledger convention (#7): when a human overrides/resolves-as-invalid a bot finding, file a bug-typed
  rebar ticket tagged fp:code-review with the finding + fingerprint + root-cause category; a periodic
  compile step turns recurring causes into eval no-fire cases / overlay guards. Manual to start.
- Grounding-effort proxy + health telemetry (#10): health-band metrics on coverage.metrics (advisory-vs-
  blocking ratio, findings/run, verify-drop rate, INDETERMINATE rate); a low-grounding observational note
  when an agentic pass makes zero tool calls on a NON-TRIVIAL diff. Define non-trivial concretely: a diff
  exceeding a small fixed threshold (proposed: > 1 changed file OR > 20 changed lines); pick one value,
  make it a named constant, and note it (never blocks).
- Approach-viability note (#12): deterministic post-Pass-3 signal (unusually high surviving-high-priority
  count or high Pass-2 drop rate) -> advisory coverage note for agent consumers, not a verdict change.

## Scope
A tags/convention doc + light tooling for the ledger; metric emission on coverage.metrics; a small
post-decide note in the verdict assembly. All advisory/observational.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] fp:code-review ledger convention documented; a compile step drafts eval cases from ledger tickets.
- [ ] Health-band metrics emitted on coverage.metrics; low-grounding note emitted using the named
      non-trivial-diff threshold (never blocks).
- [ ] Approach-viability advisory note emitted from a deterministic post-Pass-3 signal.
